titleEn: Senate farm bill draft omits Prop 12 fix for pork industry
titleZh: 美国参议院农业法案草案未纳入解决加州第12号提案的条款，猪业组织强烈呼吁
summaryZh: 美国参议院农业委员会发布的2026年农业法案讨论草案未包含解决加州第12号提案（Prop 12）的条款，该提案对猪肉生产空间标准提出严格限制，影响跨州贸易。由全国猪肉生产者委员会（NPPC）领导的330个组织联盟呼吁参议院农业委员会领导人在最终法案中加入解决方案。尽管草案包含多项养猪生产者优先事项，但缺失Prop 12修正案令行业担忧，NPPC主席表示生产者将“像依赖生计一样”继续争取修正案。此前众议院已通过类似法案，但参议院版本未采纳，且已有参议员撤回支持，肉类生产者协会投入3000万美元广告施压反对该修正案。
featuredReason: 该新闻直接关系到美国猪肉产业的跨州贸易规则与动物福利法规冲突，对智慧畜牧行业人士理解政策环境、评估市场风险及合规成本具有重要参考价值。
contentFull: Livestock ManagementFarm Policy NewsHog WelfarePork Market NewsNewsSenate farm bill draft omits Prop 12 fix for pork industryNPPC-led coalition of 330 groups is calling on Senate Agriculture Committee leaders to include a solution in the final 2026 Farm Bill.Ann Hess,Content Producer,National Hog FarmerJune 23, 2026schedule4 Min ReadGetty ImagesThe U.S. Senate Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry has released its 2026 Farm Bill discussion draft, which includes several pork producer priorities—but not a Proposition 12 fix. “We can all agree that we must take steps to help America’s farm families, and one of the most important ways we demonstrate that commitment is by passing a bipartisan farm bill,” said Senate Agriculture Committee Chairman John Boozman (R-AR). “Congress delivered historic improvements to farm programs through the Working Families Tax Cuts, and we’re continuing to expand upon that success with bipartisan priorities that strengthen the American farm economy, increase investments for rural communities and foster a more resilient agricultural sector.“I’m proud to put forward this discussion draft that reflects the input and priorities of Republicans, Democrats and most importantly, rural America. This bill is built for the people who feed America, and I look forward to continuing conversations with my colleagues about how we can best serve them and the communities they call home.”Related:Texas seeks FDA approval for feed-based ivermectinBefore the draft was released, the National Pork Producers Council led a coalition of 330 groups, urging Senate Agriculture Committee leaders to include a solution for California’s Prop 12 in the final 2026 Farm Bill.“While there is certainly room for improvement, we appreciate the Chairman putting forward a discussion draft to guide a path forward. He is spot on when he says, ‘it’s the Senate’s turn to deliver’ on a farm bill for all of rural America,” said NPPC President Rob Brenneman, a pork producer from Washington County, Iowa. “America’s pork producers will continue to advocate for a Prop 12 fix in the formal farm bill like our livelihood depends on it—because it does.”NPPC noted its appreciation for the eight Senate sponsors of the Food Security and Farm Protection Act (the Senate version of the House’s Save Our Bacon Act): Sens. Joni Ernst (R-IA), Chuck Grassley (R-IA), Kevin Cramer (R-ND), Ted Budd (R-NC), Pete Ricketts (R-NE), Thom Tillis (R-NC), John Cornyn (R-TX) and Mike Rounds (R-SD). The pork producers also acknowledged that fixing Prop 12 has broad bipartisan support, as evidenced in the U.S. House of Representatives’ 2026 Farm Bill vote. Earlier this month, another co-sponsor of the bill, Sen. Roger Marshall withdrew his support for the Food Security and Farm Protection Act. Shortly after the American Meat Producers Association allocated $30 million for a national TV, radio and digital ad campaign to pressure other members of Congress to drop the “Save Our Bacon Act” from the farm bill and to protect independent farmers’ access to Prop 12-compliant markets.Related:Strengthen FAD preparedness: 3 essential steps for pork producers"We're pleased Senator Boozman recognizes that the corrupt 'Save Our Bacon' Act is a poison pill in the farm bill and has left this dangerous provision out of his draft," said Holly Bice, CEO of the American Meat Producers Association. "The Save Our Bacon Act saves China's pork industry at the expense of American family farmers and producers."A farm bill without a Prop 12 fix also drew praise from Farm Action. "The Senate farm bill avoids some of the most harmful provisions included in the House version, including the effort to overturn state protections like California's Proposition 12,” said Angela Huffman, president of Farm Action. “That's an important win for the independent hog farmers who fought to protect this market.”NPPC noted the Senate farm bill draft did include several wins for the U.S. pork industry, such as codifying the long-standing National Poultry Improvement Plan and directing the establishment of a permanent U.S. Swine Health Improvement Plan to advance the safe and efficient trade of poultry and pork.Other key livestock areas covered in the Senate draft include:Related:Improved parity distribution may be biggest benefit of adopting skip-a-heatExpanding opportunities for critical animal health programs to receive additional funding through annual appropriations and provide clarity for allowable uses of funds under these programs, including for furthering animal disease traceability. Bolstering USDA efforts to prevent foreign animal diseases from entering the U.S. by requiring robust electronic documentation for the importation of dogs. Directing USDA to report on its readiness to support livestock producers and poultry growers facing economic losses during a FAD outbreak. Encouraging USDA and USTR to preemptively negotiate regionalization agreements with key trading partners in advance of animal disease outbreaks.Creating a narrow pathway for the online interstate sale of certain state-inspected meat and poultry products directly to consumers. Allowing livestock markets to invest in certain meat packers to support local and regional processing. Directing USDA to carry out a study on the production and marketing of livestock.Expanding the Livestock Indemnity Program to cover losses of unweaned livestock, establish weight categories to more accurately compensate producers and clarify the documentation sufficient for payments.About the AuthorAnn HessContent Producer, National Hog FarmerAnn Hess is Content Producer for the Livestock Team of Farm Progress, which includes National Hog Farmer, BEEF Magazine and Feedstuffs. A native of South Dakota, Hess has more than 21 years of experience in agriculture marketing and communications. Prior to joining the team, she worked for South Dakota State University Extension, AGDAILY and Alltech. Hess holds a bachelor's degree in journalism from South Dakota State University.See more from Ann Hesschevron_rightSubscribe to Our NewslettersNational Hog Farmer is the source for hog production, translationZh:
美国参议院农业、营养和林业委员会已发布其2026年农业法案讨论草案，其中包含多项养猪生产者优先事项——但未纳入解决加州第12号提案（Prop 12）的条款。

“我们都同意必须采取措施帮助美国的农业家庭，而展示这一承诺的最重要方式之一是通过一项两党合作的农业法案，”参议院农业委员会主席约翰·布兹曼（阿肯色州共和党人）表示。“国会通过《工薪家庭减税法案》为农业项目带来了历史性的改进，我们正在继续以两党优先事项扩大这一成功，以加强美国农业经济、增加对农村社区的投资并培育更具韧性的农业部门。

“我很自豪地提出这份讨论草案，它反映了共和党人、民主党人以及最重要的农村美国的意见和优先事项。该法案是为养活美国的人们而制定的，我期待继续与同事们讨论如何最好地为他们及其称之为家的社区服务。”

相关：得克萨斯州寻求FDA批准饲料用伊维菌素

在草案发布之前，全国猪肉生产者委员会（NPPC）领导了一个由330个组织组成的联盟，敦促参议院农业委员会领导人在最终的2026年农业法案中纳入解决加州第12号提案的解决方案。

“虽然肯定还有改进空间，但我们感谢主席提出一份讨论草案以指导前进方向。他说‘现在是参议院为整个农村美国交付农业法案的时候了’，这一点完全正确，”NPPC主席罗布·布伦内曼表示，他本人是来自爱荷华州华盛顿县的养猪生产者。“美国猪肉生产者将继续为正式农业法案中的Prop 12修正案而倡导，就像我们的生计依赖于此一样——因为确实如此。”

NPPC对《粮食安全与农场保护法案》（众议院《拯救我们的培根法案》的参议院版本）的八位参议院发起人表示感谢：参议员乔尼·恩斯特（爱荷华州共和党人）、查克·格拉斯利（爱荷华州共和党人）、凯文·克莱默（北达科他州共和党人）、泰德·巴德（北卡罗来纳州共和党人）、皮特·里基茨（内布拉斯加州共和党人）、汤姆·蒂利斯（北卡罗来纳州共和党人）、约翰·科宁（得克萨斯州共和党人）和迈克·朗兹（南达科他州共和党人）。猪肉生产者还承认，解决Prop 12问题拥有广泛的两党支持，这一点在美国众议院2026年农业法案投票中得到了证明。本月早些时候，该法案的另一位共同发起人、参议员罗杰·马歇尔撤回了对《粮食安全与农场保护法案》的支持。此后不久，美国肉类生产者协会拨款3000万美元用于全国电视、广播和数字广告活动，以施压其他国会议员放弃《拯救我们的培根法案》。
contentHtml: NPPC-led coalition of 330 groups is calling on Senate Agriculture Committee leaders to include a solution in the final 2026 Farm Bill.
